name: plan_mode
description: End-to-end plan flow: switch to Plan, ask the LLM to propose a plan, wait for the plan bubble (Approve & execute / Reject Hyperlinks), click Approve & execute, wait for the agent to run all three steps, verify hello.py landed on disk. Requires LM Studio reachable at http://127.0.0.1:1234 with a tool-calling-capable model. Pins LOCUS_GLOBAL_DIR to a fresh tmp dir so the active endpoint is the seeded default (LM Studio local) rather than whatever the developer's global endpoints.json last selected -- without this the GUI may inherit a hosted endpoint (e.g. NVIDIA Build) left active by the integration suite's endpoint-switch test and stall on a free-tier queue. Note: small models occasionally drop into 'malformed tool call' on propose_plan; if this script flakes, look at .locus/locus.log in the temp workspace for the LLM dropped-call warning -- that's the model, not the harness.

args.text: Use the propose_plan tool to plan creating a hello world Python file. Three steps: create the file, write the print statement, run it. Keep step descriptions short.
